markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Persist Monaco code block content, language, display options, diagnostics, and source links through rich document APIs. This MT belongs to TiptapMonacoIntegration. Integrate Tiptap and Monaco as the full-featured Handshake editor surface.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Tiptap/ProseMirror is the canonical rich document engine for this WP; Monaco is the canonical code-block/code editing surface embedded inside Handshake.
Tiptap and Monaco must be fully integrated into Handshake packaging, UI state, backend save/load, visual debugging, and tests; no separate external editor app may be required.
scope.expected_behavior: CodeBlockPersistenceBridge leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: CodeBlockPersistenceBridge is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

evidence: Built+verified d7e2b397: code_block_serialization.test.ts 5/5 - code-block persistence bridge (language+text+hash stable). TiptapMonacoIntegration group complete: full app vitest 60 files/442 tests green; tsc --noEmit + eslint clean; offline Playwright roundtrip PASS (bundled Monaco mounts + round-trips, ZERO external network requests, worker chunks loopback-only). RichTextEditor wired into RichDocumentView over the real rich-doc backend; no src/backend or .GOV product code touched.

evidence.finding: M9 dead integrity check + M10 single-minting-point bypass
evidence.fix: new pure verifyDocCodeBlockIntegrity walker runs on every RichDocumentView load -> typed 'integrity' banner (codeIntegrityError, new EditorBackendErrorKind); every writer (MonacoCodeBlockView listener/picker/fallback, setMonacoCodeBlockLanguage) now mints attrs via makeCodeBlockAttrs
tests: code_block_serialization.test.ts: walks a whole document and reports per-block integrity violations
code_block_serialization.test.ts: doc integrity walk passes clean docs, skips empty legacy blocks, never throws on junk
RichDocumentView.test.tsx: surfaces code-block hash violations on load as a typed integrity banner

evidence.finding: L11 hash separator ambiguity - root-caused: the separator was a LITERAL NUL byte in source (rendered invisibly as a space to every reader incl. the review)
evidence.fix: explicit � escape + documented SEPARATOR INVARIANT in codeBlockHash (language ids never contain NUL -> first NUL always delimits; hashes byte-identical, no migration); Playwright spec cross-checks with an independent FNV using the same separator
tests: rich_editor_typing.spec.ts H4/H6 cases: independent FNV equals stored contentHash in the running browser

findings.title: verifyCodeBlockIntegrity is never called by any product load path - the integrity check is dead in production
findings.evidence: grep across app/src: verifyCodeBlockIntegrity/deserializeCodeNode/serializeCodeNode appear ONLY in test files. code_block_serialization.ts:69-74 documents "compared on load to detect silent corruption ... surfaced by the load path (MT-174)", but RichDocumentView load (RichDocumentView.tsx:58-87) never verifies code-block hashes. Silent out-of-band corruption is NOT detected, contradicting the MT-168 stated purpose.
findings.fix: On document load, walk content_json monacoCodeBlock nodes and verifyCodeBlockIntegrity; surface a typed MT-174 error (kind index or a new integrity kind) listing the failing blocks; add the load-path test.

findings.title: Upstream attr writes can mint hash/language mismatches (cross-ref MT-165 stale-closure bug)
findings.evidence: The single-minting-point claim (makeCodeBlockAttrs, code_block_serialization.ts:52-65) is bypassed by MonacoCodeBlockView.tsx:73 which computes codeBlockHash(staleLanguage, next) directly. The bridge invariant (hash always derived from current language+code) is violated by its main writer.
findings.fix: Route ALL attr writes through makeCodeBlockAttrs(currentLanguage, code) (no direct codeBlockHash calls in views); fix the stale closure per MT-165 review.

findings.title: Hash input concatenation has a theoretical ambiguity
findings.evidence: codeBlockHash uses `${language} ${code}`; a language id containing a space would alias pairs. Registry ids contain no spaces today, so this is latent only.
findings.fix: Use a non-collidable separator (e.g. language.length prefix or \u0000) or document the no-space invariant where ids are minted.

parity_gaps: Round-trip integrity: persisted but unverified on load -> the §7.1.1.8 deterministic round-trip assertion is not actually enforced at runtime
missing_tests: document load with a tampered code-block hash surfaces a typed integrity error (blocked: not wired)
NodeView edit after language switch keeps hash valid (cross-ref MT-165)

wp_validator_review_v2.review_method: Integration Validator multi-agent workflow (wf_0ac25815-485 + wf_98386044-70a): independent per-MT reviewer + adversarial confirm on FAIL. No ACP. Governance/repo paperwork non-blocking per operator directive (2026-06-18). Bar = good code vs MT contract + WP packet + current Master Spec v02.194, plus the WP_VALIDATOR Spec-Realism Gate.
wp_validator_review_v2.summary: MT-168 (CodeBlockPersistenceBridge) requires persisting Monaco code-block language + code + a deterministic round-trip integrity hash through the rich-doc save API, with PostgreSQL content_sha256 as durable authority. The code implements this with a pure, unit-tested serialization bridge; all three prior adversarial findings (dead integrity check, minting-point bypass, separator ambiguity) are genuinely fixed in live product code, and a real-browser Playwright proof cross-checks the hash. Aligns with current Master Spec v02.194 EditorCodeNode round-trip-hash requirement.
findings: app/src/lib/editor/code_block_serialization.ts: pure FNV-1a serialization bridge (makeCodeBlockAttrs, serializeCodeNode, deserializeCodeNode, verifyCodeBlockIntegrity, verifyDocCodeBlockIntegrity). Deserialization tolerates partial/legacy/camelCase shapes and repairs a missing hash so a load never throws. Real backend authority is PostgreSQL content_sha256 (knowledge_documents.rs:334/376) via existing /knowledge/documents/:id/save API; no new SQLite or forbidden tech introduced.

Prior MEDIUM M9 (integrity check dead in production) is FIXED: verifyDocCodeBlockIntegrity is called on every load at RichDocumentView.tsx:477 and surfaces a typed 'integrity' banner via codeIntegrityError (backend_error.ts:118, kind 'integrity'). Editing stays possible; backend content_sha256 remains durable authority.

Prior MEDIUM M10 (minting-point bypass) is FIXED: all MonacoCodeBlockView writers (lines 235, 351, 367) route through makeCodeBlockAttrs; node node-builder, editor_commands, auto_code_block_rules, document_diff_merge all use makeCodeBlockAttrs. The one remaining direct codeBlockHash call (find_replace.ts:249) re-mints from current language+code consistently with the invariant, so it is not a corruption bypass.

Prior LOW L11 (separator ambiguity) is FIXED: codeBlockHash uses explicit � separator with a documented SEPARATOR INVARIANT (code_block_serialization.ts:40-58); byte-identical hash output, no migration.

Sub-rule 1 clean: no todo!/unimplemented!/'deferred to follow-on'/'pending MT-'/Unavailable placeholders reachable from required behavior; the only 'scaffold' grep hit is a benign comment describing the offline static-server test harness.

Sub-rule 2 satisfied: real browser proof in tests/dependency_policy/rich_editor_typing.spec.ts H4 (line 290) drives real Monaco via page.keyboard, performs a language switch, and independently recomputes the product FNV hash (same � separator + 0x811c9dc5/0x01000193 constants, line 79-83) against the live contentHash in the running editor. Not a mock or tautology; H7 in commit 7f8c9f34 additionally re-hydrates through the real ProseMirror schema (node.check()).

Spec alignment: current Master Spec v02.194 02-system-architecture.md:2708 defines EditorCodeNode with a 'round-trip hash' and line 2719 requires 'RichDocument round-trip with EditorCodeNode' in the vertical slice. Implementation matches.
Aside (non-blocking, expected): contract cites v02.192 anchor 7.1.1.8 / lifecycle paperwork; resolved against current v02.194 with no behavioral discrepancy.

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
